name: Browser Tools
description: Control a real browser via Chrome DevTools MCP — navigate, click, fill forms, take screenshots, debug, and more
field.headless.description: Run Chrome in headless mode (no visible window). Default: false.
systemPrompt:
When the user needs to interact with a web page, scrape content, take screenshots, fill forms, debug, or automate browser tasks, use the Browser Tools extension powered by Chrome DevTools MCP.

Setup: If this is the first time, call `browser_setup` to configure the MCP connection. Once configured, the extension registers a chrome-devtools MCP server automatically.

Element selection: Chrome DevTools MCP uses UIDs (not CSS selectors) to identify elements. Always call `take_snapshot` first to get element UIDs, then pass UIDs to `click`, `fill`, `hover`, etc.

Workflow: Use `browser_setup` if not configured. Then use `ToolSearch` with `source:"mcp"` to discover the `mcp__chrome-devtools__*` tools. Call `mcp__chrome-devtools__navigate_page` to open a URL, `mcp__chrome-devtools__take_snapshot` to get element UIDs, and `mcp__chrome-devtools__click` / `mcp__chrome-devtools__fill` to interact. Use `mcp__chrome-devtools__take_screenshot` to visually verify. Use `mcp__chrome-devtools__list_console_messages` and `mcp__chrome-devtools__list_network_requests` to debug. Clean up with `mcp__chrome-devtools__close_page` when done.

Visible browser: Chrome DevTools MCP starts a visible browser window by default. All operations happen in real time in the browser window. No extra configuration needed.

setup.title: Set up Browser Tools
setup.description: Configure the Chrome DevTools MCP connection.
setup.prompt: Set up Browser Tools for Finch.

scrape-webpage.title: Scrape a webpage
scrape-webpage.description: Navigate to a URL and extract its readable content.
scrape-webpage.prompt: Navigate to https://example.com and extract the page content.
